skillName: Use formal methods for subtraction of integers
misconceptionSummary: Students subtract the smaller digit from the larger within a column regardless of which is on top.

lines.text: Align columns, larger number on top
Work right to left: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands

steps.narration: Write the larger number on top, smaller below, with digits aligned by column. Subtract from right to left — ones first, then tens, hundreds, thousands.

lines.text: Borrow 1 ten: ones becomes 11, tens reduces by 1

steps.narration: When the top digit is smaller, borrow one from the next column. One ten becomes ten ones added to the current ones digit. Eleven minus seven is four.

Continue column by column, borrowing whenever the top digit is smaller. After the borrow the tens digit was reduced to one — one minus four requires another borrow, giving eleven minus four equals seven.

visuals.label: Align → subtract right to left → borrow when top < bottom

steps.narration: Two thousand two hundred and seventy-four. Borrow only when needed, always reducing the column you borrowed from by one.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: subtracting the smaller digit from the larger regardless of position — writing 7 − 1 = 6 in the ones column instead of borrowing to get 11 − 7 = 4.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Always subtract top minus bottom. If the top digit is smaller, borrow from the next column — never flip the subtraction to avoid a negative.
